# MiddleLeap

> MiddleLeap is an independent advisory firm based in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. It helps banks, fintechs, financial infrastructure providers and regulated platform businesses move from strategic mandate to market execution.

Canonical website: https://middleleap.com/
Contact: contact@middleleap.com
Founded: 2021
Founder: Michael Ryberg Hartmann
Primary market: Middle East and North Africa

## Positioning

MiddleLeap helps leaders turn regulatory, commercial and technology shifts into platform advantage. Its work connects market position, platform economics, ecosystem design and AI-native operating models. Open Finance is a significant area of experience, but it is one part of the broader regulated platform transformation practice.

## Core capabilities

- Regulatory and market transformation: Open Finance strategy, regulatory readiness, LFI and TPP operating models, market entry and ecosystem participation.
- Platform and ecosystem strategy: platform propositions, API strategy, embedded finance, partnerships, commercial models and monetisation.
- AI-native operating models: value-stream redesign, agentic workflows, product and engineering models, governance and control.
- Transformation and execution: executive mobilisation, programme leadership, platform delivery, organization design and delivery modernisation.

## How MiddleLeap works

MiddleLeap works from mandate to working capability through five stages:

1. Frame the regulatory, commercial or operating mandate.
2. Design the proposition, ecosystem, platform and operating model.
3. Mobilise leaders, partners, product teams and governance stakeholders.
4. Deliver working products and capabilities.
5. Codify reusable knowledge, controls and agent workflows.

The Loom is MiddleLeap's governed AI delivery harness. Its shared core combines evidence-led discovery, bounded AI-assisted delivery and human authority. A regulated profile adds formal assurance, audit, lineage and institutional controls; a venture profile applies commercial-evidence, product-safety and expert-authority gates.

The Loom is the governed delivery method. The Loom Toolkit is the installable product that carries the method, controls and adoption machinery into a repository. AI-DLC is the technical distribution mechanism for the Toolkit rather than a separate consulting proposition. The Institutional BrainKit is the institution-owned context component, and domain packs are optional specialist intelligence.

### The Loom

- Discovery runs through gates D1-D9, covering framing, evidence, scope, the no-solutioning boundary, synthesis, data governance, brand conformance, tangibility and stakeholder validation.
- Delivery is spec-first and agent-enabled, with bounded reviewers, quality gates, tripwire hooks and a merge policy in which agents propose and accountable humans approve.
- The context brain has three dimensions: regulated context, solution-domain knowledge and institutional DNA.
- The first evidenced implementation was the UAE Open Finance Back Office, where 134 of approximately 139 stories reached done under quality gates, synthetic-data constraints and human four-eyes merge.
- The current proof is demo-proven, not production-proven. It has not cleared live production scale or a regulator examination.

### The Loom Toolkit

- Core Toolkit: regulated discovery, delivery, continuous assurance, harness governance, repository foundations and the Institutional BrainKit.
- Institutional BrainKit: a private, human-approved package of institutional terminology, architecture, technology policy, design standards and decision rights.
- Open Finance domain pack: UAE standards, Islamic-banking context, risk review and value-proposition prototyping.
- AI-DLC: the repository and plugin mechanism used to distribute, version and adopt the public Toolkit.

## Ventures, portfolio and studio

MiddleLeap builds and contributes to working platforms so its advisory work is grounded in operating evidence. The primary portfolio contains one flagship regulated proof and two venture experiments:

- Open Finance Backoffice: the flagship regulated delivery proof, implemented with synthetic data and the regulated Loom profile.
- Parqo: a venture experiment using commercial evidence to gate investment in a two-sided parking marketplace.
- HiveMind / Hive Coach: a service venture experiment testing how AI can extend an expert service while preserving human authority.

OpenFinance-OS, the Data Sandbox and Ecosystem Watcher are presented separately as independent ecosystem contributions. MiddleLeap's role is contribution and stewardship, not institutional ownership.

The MiddleLeap Venture Studio invites operators, domain experts and potential partners to propose non-confidential, evidenced problems in regulated markets, platform businesses and financial infrastructure. Submissions are screened before any evidence sprint, venture thesis or bounded pilot.

The portfolio operates as a build, learn and apply loop: build working assets, capture market and execution intelligence, and apply the learning to client mandates.

## Engagement models

- Executive advisory for regulatory, platform and transformation mandates.
- Platform strategy sprints covering proposition, ecosystem, commercial model and execution roadmap.
- AI-native delivery pilots that adopt The Loom around one bounded outcome and leave a governed repository capability behind.

## Experience and delivery model

- MiddleLeap is a founder-led, networked advisory practice rather than a fixed consulting bench.
- Each mandate has senior accountability, specialists assembled around the problem and active client leadership so institutional context and delivery capability remain with the client.
- Experience carried into the practice includes building and expanding an Open Banking platform across MENA, followed by leadership of a dual LFI/TPP programme that helped a leading UAE bank achieve first-bank certification under the UAE Open Finance framework and deliver the country's first live transactions with a licensed TPP.
- Business-banking ecosystem experience includes the build-out of Danske Bank's District platform and marketplace across the Nordics and UK, the migration of 250,000 organizations and the development of API-based partner channels.
- Enterprise transformation experience includes leadership of a 70+ person digital delivery organization through an API-first Telco-as-a-Service shift within a 140-year-old company.
- MiddleLeap was founded by Michael Ryberg Hartmann, a product and technology executive with more than 20 years across banking, fintech, telecommunications and SaaS. He leads selected mandates and assembles the senior specialists required around each client and problem.
- Professional profile: https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-ryberg-hartmann

## Primary source

- [MiddleLeap homepage](https://middleleap.com/): current positioning, capabilities, approach, experience and contact information.
- [MiddleLeap Ventures](https://middleleap.com/ventures): portfolio, ecosystem contributions, project status and Venture Studio entry point.
- [Venture Studio](https://middleleap.com/ventures/studio): assessment criteria, evidence-led process and proposal route.
- [The Loom](https://middleleap.com/the-loom): governed AI delivery harness, regulated and venture profiles, context brain, assurance and stated limits.
- [The Loom Toolkit](https://middleleap.com/ai-dlc): installable governed delivery system, Institutional BrainKit, AI-DLC distribution mechanism and optional domain intelligence.
- [Open Finance Back Office build record](https://middleleap.com/ventures/backoffice): evidence-backed architecture, AI delivery harness, technology stack, quality system and development maturity.
- [HiveMind build record](https://middleleap.com/ventures/hivemind): the human-led Hive Coach product, runtime AI pipeline, delivery harness, quality system and development maturity.
- [Parqo build record](https://middleleap.com/ventures/parqo): the live acquisition wedge, approved marketplace design, AI-native delivery controls and next validation gates.

Last reviewed: 2026-07-24
